prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens with a very close, low-resolution shot of a man’s face. The image is dark and heavily affected by video artifacts and color banding, making facial details unclear.

It then cuts to close-up views of a tabletop electronic device. The device has a rectangular top surface with a central slot or opening and a row of light-colored rectangular buttons across the front. The shot emphasizes the controls and mechanical-looking top section.

Next, the device is shown from farther back on a table. A hand reaches toward or presses controls on the top/front of the unit. Behind it, a small television screen displays a football game. White on-screen text appears near the bottom; it is partially legible and reads “ACTUAL CLOSED-CIRCUIT TV PICTURE” and “TWO HOUR RECORDING TIME.”

The commercial then shows another close-up of the device’s right-side control area. There are sliding switches or levers on the top panel and stacked round knobs or dials on the front right side, suggesting adjustable settings.

A profile shot follows of a blond-haired man in a checked shirt and dark sweater. He is shown from the side against a dark background, looking downward or toward something off-screen. No interaction is visible in this sampled frame.

The final frame is a product display shot against a dark studio background. The electronic unit sits centered on a reflective surface, with another component or cover positioned behind it. Above the product is the “Zenith” logo and the printed slogan: “The quality goes in before the name goes on.”
